calculation_logic: abs(move_2d_pct) / atr_pct where atr_pct = Wilder RMA 20-day ATR pre-event
plain_english_definition: ATR-normalised absolute stock move over 2 days after event
why_it_matters: Primary regression target — size of move expressed in units of pre-event volatility
notes: 2 nulls from ATR=0 failure; primary continuous target

calculation_logic: MOVE_BUCKET_MAP: Noise=0, Low=1, Medium=2, High=3, Extreme=4
plain_english_definition: Ordinal move class: 0=Noise, 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Extreme
why_it_matters: Primary classification target — 5-class ordinal label for move magnitude

calculation_logic: 1 if move_class_norm in {High, Extreme}, else 0
plain_english_definition: Binary flag: did this event produce a High or Extreme ATR-normalised move?
why_it_matters: Binary classification target — identifies outlier-magnitude catalyst reactions
notes: 100% populated; useful for imbalanced binary classifier

calculation_logic: PHASE_MAP: Phase1(Early)=0.5, Phase1=1.0, Phase1/2=1.5, Phase2=2.0, Phase2/3=2.5, Phase3=3.0, Phase4=4.0
plain_english_definition: Numeric encoding of clinical trial phase
why_it_matters: Phase is one of the strongest drivers of move magnitude — late-stage trials move markets more
validity_rule: null for rows without nct_id (no CT.gov match, 53 rows)
notes: impute with mode (3.0) for baseline model

calculation_logic: 1.0 if feat_phase_num >= 2.5, else 0.0; NaN if phase unknown
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial is Phase 2/3 or later; 0 otherwise
why_it_matters: Simplified binary late-stage signal — useful when phase encoding is too granular

calculation_logic: MESH_ENCODE_MAP: Neoplasms=1, NervousSystem=2, Immune=3, Endocrine=4, Respiratory=5, Infectious=6, Cardiovascular=7, GI=8, Skin=9, Musculoskeletal=10, Other=11
plain_english_definition: Integer code for disease category from MeSH taxonomy Level-1
why_it_matters: Disease area is a core stratifier — oncology and CNS events have structurally different move distributions

calculation_logic: MESH_TO_SUPERCLASS dict: Neoplasms→Oncology, NervousSystem→CNS, etc.
plain_english_definition: Human-readable therapeutic area label: Oncology / CNS / Immunology / etc.
why_it_matters: More readable version of mesh_level1_encoded; useful for grouping, stratification, display
notes: use for display and reaction prior grouping; same grouping as mesh_level1_encoded

calculation_logic: 1 if mesh_level1 == 'Neoplasms', else 0
plain_english_definition: 1 if this is an oncology event
why_it_matters: Oncology (28% of dataset) has distinct market dynamics — often larger moves and different endpoint standards

calculation_logic: 1 if mesh_level1 == 'Nervous System Diseases', else 0
plain_english_definition: 1 if this is a CNS/neurology event
why_it_matters: CNS (20%) has high clinical and market uncertainty — distinct move prior from oncology

calculation_logic: 1 if feat_orphan_flag==1 OR rare-disease keywords in indication/mesh_level1
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial targets a rare/orphan disease
why_it_matters: Rare disease trials have smaller patient populations and often greater market sensitivity per result
notes: derived from orphan designation; 2 rows flagged

calculation_logic: log10(market_cap_m.clip(lower=0.001))
plain_english_definition: Log-base-10 of market capitalisation in USD millions
why_it_matters: Market cap is a fundamental size feature — small caps move more on the same clinical news
validity_rule: non-null; market_cap_m clipped at 0.001 to avoid log(0)

calculation_logic: atr_pct = Wilder RMA ewm(alpha=1/20, adjust=False) 20-day pre-event ATR as % of price
plain_english_definition: Pre-event 20-day Average True Range as % of stock price (Wilder method)
why_it_matters: Pre-event volatility directly scales the expected move size — core feature for normalising targets
notes: 2 nulls; impute with median for baseline

calculation_logic: 1.0 if short_percent >= 20, else 0.0; NaN if unknown
plain_english_definition: 1 if short interest ≥ 20% of float at time of event
why_it_matters: High short interest amplifies moves — positive catalysts can trigger short squeezes

calculation_logic: 1.0 if institutional_ownership < 30%, else 0.0; NaN if unknown
plain_english_definition: 1 if institutional ownership is below 30%
why_it_matters: Low institutional ownership indicates retail-dominated float — higher volatility and gap risk

calculation_logic: Nano (<50M), Micro (50-300M), Small (300M-2B), Mid (2-10B), Large (>10B)
plain_english_definition: Market cap size tier label
why_it_matters: Enables non-linear market-cap stratification and group-level reaction priors
notes: used as grouping key for reaction priors

calculation_logic: cash_position_m / market_cap_m, clipped at 5.0
plain_english_definition: Cash holdings as a fraction of market cap — proxy for balance-sheet buffer
why_it_matters: Companies where cash > market cap (>1.0) are near existential crisis; amplifies move sensitivity
validity_rule: non-null if both inputs present; clipped at 5.0

calculation_logic: groupby(ticker).transform('count') on all rows
plain_english_definition: Total number of clinical catalyst events for this company in the dataset
why_it_matters: Proxy for company maturity — more events = more established clinical program
notes: 100% coverage; reflects dataset history, not full CT.gov pipeline

calculation_logic: count of distinct drug_name values per ticker
plain_english_definition: Number of distinct drugs this company has clinical events for
why_it_matters: Pipeline breadth indicator — 1 drug = existential concentration risk

calculation_logic: 1 if n_unique_drugs_for_company <= 1, else 0
plain_english_definition: 1 if the company has only one drug with clinical events in the dataset
why_it_matters: Single-asset companies face binary outcomes — catalyst reactions tend to be more extreme

calculation_logic: 1drug→1.0, 2drugs→0.7, 3-4drugs→0.4, 5+drugs→0.2
plain_english_definition: Score (0.2–1.0) reflecting how dependent the company is on its lead drug
why_it_matters: Higher score = more existential — a positive or negative catalyst on a single drug is more market-moving

calculation_logic: count of events per ticker where feat_phase_num >= 2.5
plain_english_definition: Number of late-stage (Phase 2/3+) catalyst events for this company
why_it_matters: Late-stage trial count is a pipeline maturity signal

calculation_logic: lead_asset_dependency_score × (n_late_stage_trials / n_trials_for_company)
plain_english_definition: Composite: how much of a company's trial activity is concentrated in late-stage assets
why_it_matters: High concentration = late-stage binary events dominate the company's clinical narrative

calculation_logic: groupby([ticker, drug_name_normalised]).transform('count')
plain_english_definition: Number of dataset events for this specific drug within this company
why_it_matters: Distinguishes between a drug with one data point vs one with multiple readouts — the latter has richer prior

calculation_logic: asset_trial_count_for_company / n_trials_for_company
plain_english_definition: Fraction of all company events that belong to this drug
why_it_matters: High share = this drug dominates the company's clinical history in the dataset

calculation_logic: min(n_late_stage_trials, 5) × 0.6 + log1p(n_unique_drugs) × 0.4
plain_english_definition: Composite pipeline depth score combining late-stage breadth and drug count
why_it_matters: Richer pipeline = market already has priced in some success probability; lower marginal move per trial

source_columns: pivotal_evidence, v_summary, v_pr_key_info, catalyst_summary, feat_nda_bla_flag, feat_priority_review_flag
calculation_logic: 0=no regulatory language, 1=pivotal/registrational keywords, 2=NDA/BLA filed, 3=priority review; cumulative max
plain_english_definition: Ordinal score (0–3) for regulatory significance of this trial event
why_it_matters: Pivotal/regulatory-stage events move markets more than exploratory Phase 2 data — key importance signal
notes: 100% coverage; already captures is_pivotal signal better than raw column

source_columns: feat_phase_num, feat_regulatory_stage_score, feat_breakthrough_flag, feat_priority_review_flag, pivotal_evidence, v_summary, v_pr_key_info
calculation_logic: +1.5 phase>=3, +1.0 phase==2.5 or reg_score>=2, +0.5 reg_score==1, +0.5 BTD, +0.5 priority_review, +0.5 pivotal text keywords; max ~5.0
plain_english_definition: Composite pivotal importance score (0–5) combining phase, regulatory status, designations, and text evidence
why_it_matters: More complete importance signal than any single flag — synthesises all available pivotal evidence into one score
notes: 100% coverage; replaces raw is_pivotal (54% null); mean=1.12, 310 rows score >=2.0

calculation_logic: 1 if orphan designation keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if orphan drug designation mentioned in trial evidence
why_it_matters: Orphan designation signals rare disease focus and sometimes expedited regulatory pathway

calculation_logic: 1 if fast track designation keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if FDA Fast Track Designation mentioned
why_it_matters: Fast Track expedites review and signals FDA acknowledgment of unmet need

calculation_logic: 1 if Breakthrough Therapy Designation keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation mentioned
why_it_matters: BTD is the highest FDA designation — strong signal of expected efficacy and expedited pathway

calculation_logic: 1 if NDA/BLA submission or approval keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if NDA or BLA filing/approval event
why_it_matters: NDA/BLA events are approval-stage — highest commercial significance; typically large moves

calculation_logic: 1 if Priority Review designation keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if FDA Priority Review granted
why_it_matters: Priority Review shortens FDA review to 6 months — confirms regulatory importance of the drug

calculation_logic: Yes=+1.0, No=-1.0, Unclear/missing=0.0. Does NOT use is_pivotal.
plain_english_definition: Endpoint outcome score: +1 if primary endpoint met, -1 if failed, 0 if unknown
why_it_matters: Cleanest direct measure of trial success — avoids leaking is_pivotal sparsity into the score
notes: Replaces deprecated feat_endpoint_positive_score. 308 rows with non-zero value (37%).

calculation_logic: 1 if primary_endpoint_met in {'yes','no'} (case-insensitive), else 0
plain_english_definition: 1 if the primary endpoint outcome is known (either Yes or No)
why_it_matters: Knowing whether the endpoint was hit is itself predictive — rows with unknown outcome behave differently

calculation_logic: 1 if superiority / met-primary / positive-result keywords found across evidence text fields
plain_english_definition: 1 if evidence text suggests the trial met its primary endpoint or showed superiority
why_it_matters: Direct positive evidence signal — even before structured endpoint data is present
notes: 331 rows (40%); 19 rows also have feat_mixed_results_flag=1 (ambiguous results)

calculation_logic: 1 if p-value / hazard ratio / confidence interval keywords found in evidence text
plain_english_definition: 1 if statistically significant results reported (p-value, HR, CI mentioned)
why_it_matters: Quantitative evidence of efficacy — presence of stats language is itself a positive signal

calculation_logic: 1 if clinically meaningful / durable / complete response keywords found
plain_english_definition: 1 if evidence mentions clinically meaningful or durable outcomes
why_it_matters: Distinguishes statistical significance from clinical impact — a key driver of market reaction

calculation_logic: 1 if mixed / partial / did-not-meet / failed-primary keywords found
plain_english_definition: 1 if evidence suggests mixed, partial, or failed primary endpoint results
why_it_matters: Mixed results produce attenuated moves — important for distinguishing full success from ambiguous outcomes

calculation_logic: +2 RANDOMIZED, +2 enrollment>300, +1 enrollment 101-300, +1 Phase3+, +0.5 Phase2/3, -1 NON_RANDOMIZED; NaN if all inputs missing
plain_english_definition: Trial design quality score (0–6) based on randomisation, enrollment size, and phase
why_it_matters: Higher quality design = more credible result = market responds more decisively
validity_rule: NaN if ct_allocation, ct_enrollment, and feat_phase_num all null

source_columns: feat_design_quality_score, v_summary, catalyst_summary, feat_breakthrough_flag, feat_withdrawn_flag, feat_terminated_flag
calculation_logic: feat_design_quality_score + 1.0×blinded_title + 0.5×breakthrough − 2.0×withdrawn − 2.0×terminated
plain_english_definition: Composite trial quality score integrating design, blinding, designations, and trial disruption
why_it_matters: Penalises withdrawn/terminated trials and rewards blinded designs — more comprehensive than design quality alone

calculation_logic: 1.0 if ct_allocation == 'RANDOMIZED', 0.0 if NON_RANDOMIZED; NaN if unknown
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial is randomised (RCT)
why_it_matters: Randomisation is the gold standard — RCT results are more market-credible than single-arm studies
notes: 160 nulls (19.3%) — missing for non-CT.gov rows and older trials

calculation_logic: 1 if controlled/comparator arm keywords found in design information
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial has a control arm (placebo or active comparator)
why_it_matters: Controlled trials produce more interpretable and market-credible results

calculation_logic: 1 if double/single-blind or masking keywords found in trial title or design text
plain_english_definition: 1 if the trial is blinded (single or double-blind)
why_it_matters: Blinded trials reduce bias — market gives more weight to blinded results

calculation_logic: 1 if open-label or unblinded keywords found in trial title or design text
plain_english_definition: 1 if the trial is open-label (no blinding)
why_it_matters: Open-label results are less credible to the market — often leads to muted reactions
notes: 238 rows (29%); not mutually exclusive with feat_blinded_flag at text level

calculation_logic: log10(ct_enrollment + 1)
plain_english_definition: Log of trial enrollment size
why_it_matters: Larger trials are more powered and credible — log scale compresses outliers

calculation_logic: 1.0 if ct_enrollment < 50, else 0.0; NaN if enrollment unknown
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial enrolled fewer than 50 participants
why_it_matters: Very small trials are early-stage feasibility studies — market reactions tend to be smaller

calculation_logic: 1 if ct_status == 'WITHDRAWN'
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial was withdrawn before any participants enrolled
why_it_matters: Withdrawal is a strong negative signal — penalises trial quality score

calculation_logic: 1 if ct_status == 'TERMINATED'
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial was terminated early (after enrolment began)
why_it_matters: Termination is a major negative event — safety signals or futility

calculation_logic: future_far (>365d), future_near (0–365d), just_completed (0 to −180d), past (<−180d), unknown
plain_english_definition: Categorical label for timing of event relative to primary completion
why_it_matters: Bucket captures non-linear timing effects: events just after completion are data readouts; far-future events are interim
notes: 100% coverage; unknown for 47 rows with missing completion date

calculation_logic: 1.0 if ct_status == 'ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING', else 0.0; NaN if status missing
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial is active but no longer enrolling — often means data collection is complete
why_it_matters: This status suggests results are imminent — timing signal for expected data readout

calculation_logic: 1.0 if ct_status == 'COMPLETED', else 0.0; NaN if status missing
plain_english_definition: 1 if the trial has officially completed
why_it_matters: Completed trials have full data — events on completed trials are final result announcements

calculation_logic: 1 if ct_status==COMPLETED AND 0 <= (event_date − ct_primary_completion).days <= 365
plain_english_definition: 1 if trial completed within 12 months before the event date
why_it_matters: Recently completed trials produce the most market-relevant data releases — highest signal quality

calculation_logic: groupby(feat_phase_num)[target_abs_move_atr].transform('mean') — computed on full dataset
plain_english_definition: Mean ATR-normalised absolute move for all events at the same trial phase
why_it_matters: Phase-level base rate for move size — a Phase 3 readout historically moves more than Phase 1
notes: ⚠ TRAIN-ONLY PRIOR: must be recomputed on training folds to avoid leakage; 53 nulls

calculation_logic: groupby(mesh_level1)[target_abs_move_atr].transform('mean')
plain_english_definition: Mean ATR-normalised absolute move for all events in the same disease category
why_it_matters: Disease-area base rate — oncology and CNS events have different historical move distributions
notes: ⚠ TRAIN-ONLY PRIOR: must be recomputed on training folds to avoid leakage

calculation_logic: groupby([feat_phase_num, mesh_level1])[target_abs_move_atr].transform('mean')
plain_english_definition: Mean ATR-normalised absolute move for events at same phase AND disease category
why_it_matters: Most specific reaction prior — e.g. Phase 3 Oncology has a different base rate than Phase 3 CNS
notes: ⚠ TRAIN-ONLY PRIOR: must be recomputed on training folds to avoid leakage

calculation_logic: groupby(feat_market_cap_bucket)[target_abs_move_atr].transform('mean')
plain_english_definition: Mean ATR-normalised absolute move for companies of similar size
why_it_matters: Small-cap companies historically have larger ATR-normalised moves than large caps on same news
notes: ⚠ TRAIN-ONLY PRIOR: must be recomputed on training folds to avoid leakage

calculation_logic: 1 if v_action in {'DATE_FIXED', 'FIX_DATE'}, else 0
plain_english_definition: 1 if event date was corrected during validation (original date was wrong)
why_it_matters: Rows with corrected dates may have price fields computed on wrong date — useful audit flag for model QA

calculation_logic: Yes+1 / No-1 / unclear 0 + is_pivotal True+1 — range -1 to +2
plain_english_definition: DEPRECATED. Mixed endpoint outcome + pivotal importance into one score.
why_it_matters: Removed because is_pivotal was 54% null, silently biasing 438 rows to piv_score=0
notes: Replaced by feat_endpoint_outcome_score (outcome only) + feat_regulatory_stage_score (importance). DO NOT use.

calculation_logic: Raw boolean from LLM extraction
plain_english_definition: EXCLUDED from baseline. Raw LLM-extracted flag for whether trial is pivotal.
why_it_matters: 54% null (376/827 populated). Silently treats null as False, biasing 438 rows.
validity_rule: present in dataset but EXCLUDED from feature matrix

description: Days from event date to ct_primary_completion date; negative = event after completion
1 if primary completion is within next 0–30 days from event date; 0 otherwise
1 if primary completion is within next 0–90 days from event date; 0 otherwise
Categorical: imminent_0_30 / near1_90 / medium_91_180 / far_180_plus / past / unknown
Days since prior clinical event for same ticker; NaN for first company event
Days since prior clinical event for same ticker+drug_name; NaN for first asset event
Ordinal position of this event within ticker+drug_name history (1=first known)
Ordinal position of this event within the company (ticker) history
1 if this company had a prior catalyst within the last 90 days; else 0
1 if this ticker+drug had a prior event within 180 days; else 0

calculation_logic: primaryCompletionDateStruct.date from CT.gov study record
plain_english_definition: Primary completion date as returned by CT.gov API (YYYY-MM-DD)
why_it_matters: Ground-truth date from registry rather than dataset field; more reliable for timing calculations
description: Primary completion date string from CT.gov API

calculation_logic: (event_anchor_date - lastUpdatePostDateStruct.date).days
plain_english_definition: Days between CT.gov last-update date and the event anchor date
why_it_matters: Freshly updated studies may signal imminent data release or amendment
description: Days since CT.gov record was last updated (relative to event anchor)

calculation_logic: 1 if feat_days_since_ctgov_last_update <= 90 else 0
plain_english_definition: Flag: CT.gov record updated within 90 days of the event
why_it_matters: Recent updates often precede results publications or study amendments
description: Binary flag: CT.gov record updated within 90 days of event anchor

calculation_logic: overallStatus from CT.gov study record at fetch time
plain_english_definition: Current trial status as of CT.gov fetch (e.g., COMPLETED, RECRUITING)
why_it_matters: Current status differs from historical status at event date; shows trial trajectory
notes: Status at time of data pull
description: CT.gov overall status at time of data pull

calculation_logic: 1 if status=COMPLETED and completion date is in the past, or status=RECRUITING and completion date in future
plain_english_definition: Flag: trial status is consistent with its completion date (sanity check)
why_it_matters: Inconsistency may signal data quality issue or unusual trial trajectory
description: Binary flag: CT.gov status is consistent with its completion date

calculation_logic: Count of RECRUITING/ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING/ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION/NOT_YET_RECRUITING interventional trials (up to 100)
plain_english_definition: Number of active interventional trials for this sponsor on CT.gov
why_it_matters: Active pipeline size signals company stage and resources
notes: Capped at 100 per query; large sponsors may be underestimated
description: Count of active/recruiting interventional CT.gov trials for sponsor

calculation_logic: Count of Phase 2, 3, 4, 2/3, 3/4 interventional trials for sponsor
plain_english_definition: Number of late-stage (Phase 2+) trials for this sponsor
why_it_matters: Late-stage trial depth correlates with development experience and probability of sophisticated trial design
description: Count of Phase 2+ interventional trials on CT.gov for sponsor

calculation_logic: Count of COMPLETED interventional trials for sponsor
plain_english_definition: Number of completed interventional trials for this sponsor
why_it_matters: Completion rate is a proxy for execution track record
description: Count of completed interventional CT.gov trials for sponsor

calculation_logic: Total interventional trials for sponsor (any status/phase, totalCount from CT.gov)
plain_english_definition: Total count of interventional CT.gov trials for this sponsor
why_it_matters: Total pipeline size — basis for maturity score denominator
notes: Uses CT.gov totalCount; large pharma sponsors (Novartis=2711) will inflate mean
description: Total interventional trials on CT.gov for sponsor (any status)

calculation_logic: (n_late + 0.5 * n_active) / sqrt(min(n_total,100) + 1)
plain_english_definition: Sponsor pipeline maturity: weighted late-stage + active trials, scaled by total pipeline size (capped at 100)
why_it_matters: Captures both depth (late-stage proportion) and breadth (active trials) in one score; higher = more mature pipeline
notes: n_sample used for denominator to avoid large-pharma distortion
description: Composite sponsor pipeline maturity: (late + 0.5*active)/sqrt(n_sample+1)

calculation_logic: Count of all interventional trials mentioning the same drug (query.intr, totalCount)
plain_english_definition: Number of CT.gov trials that include the same drug/intervention
why_it_matters: Widely-studied assets have richer clinical evidence base; affects regulatory probability
notes: Drug name normalised before query; common drug names (aspirin) return 30k+
description: Count of all CT.gov interventional trials for same drug/intervention

calculation_logic: Count of Phase 2/3+ interventional trials for same drug (from sample up to 100)
plain_english_definition: Number of late-stage (Phase 2+) CT.gov trials for this drug
why_it_matters: Drug assets with late-stage history are more likely to have established efficacy signals
notes: Based on sample of up to 100 most relevant results
description: Count of Phase 2+ CT.gov trials for same drug

calculation_logic: n_late_stage_drug / sqrt(min(n_total_drug,100) + 1)
plain_english_definition: Drug asset maturity: fraction of trials that are late-stage, scaled by total trials (capped)
why_it_matters: Mature assets (high late-stage/total ratio) have de-risked development history
notes: 0 for novel assets with no prior trials
description: Composite drug asset maturity: late_stage_drug / sqrt(min(total_drug,100)+1)
